topic: adopting renewable energy at the company level
agent_position: Your company should transition to 100% renewable energy within five years.
scenario_context: You are speaking with Jordan, the CFO of a mid-sized manufacturing firm. Jordan is analytical, numbers-driven, and deeply skeptical of initiatives without a clear ROI. Jordan has seen greenwashing before and is unimpressed by vague environmental arguments.
npc_opening_statement: Look, I'm not against the idea in principle, but I need to see hard numbers. What's the actual payback period? Because our current energy contracts are locked in and switching costs aren't trivial.
difficulty_explanation: Medium — the NPC responds strongly to LOGICAL (0.22) and CONCESSION (0.14), giving the model two clear paths. Starting at -0.5 with threshold 0.25 requires covering 0.75 points, achievable in 5-6 good turns.
benchmarking_value: Baseline scenario for testing whether a model can detect and exploit a data-driven NPC. Models that lead with evidence should win; models that lead with emotion will struggle.

positive: That's a useful case study. What was their initial capital outlay and what's the current run rate?
Interesting example. If they're genuinely saving that much, I'd want to talk to their CFO directly.
That's a specific enough example on {topic} that I can actually evaluate it. Tell me more about how it played out.
If someone in a situation like mine dealt with {topic} that way and it worked, I'd want to understand what they did differently.
A concrete case on {topic} is more useful than abstract arguments. That example is worth examining.
neutral: One example isn't a pattern. What's the distribution across similar companies?
Anecdotes are not data. I need more than one success story.
One example on {topic} doesn't make a pattern. I'd need to see whether that case generalizes.
The anecdote about {topic} is interesting, but I've seen similar stories that didn't pan out.

resistant: I could find you a counter-example for every example you give me.
Individual cases don't generalize. Our situation is different enough that this doesn't apply.
I could give you a counter-example on {topic} for every success story you tell me.
Stories about {topic} are easy to cherry-pick. Show me the full distribution of outcomes.
Anecdotes on {topic} are the most unreliable form of evidence. I need more than that.

positive: If that's what the Goldman analysis shows, I'd want to read the full report.

neutral: I'd want to see the methodology. Expert consensus can be wrong, especially on cost projections.

Experts on {topic} have been wrong before. I'd want to see the methodology, not just the conclusion.
Which experts specifically are you citing on {topic}? There's a lot of advocacy dressed up as research.
Authority on {topic} matters, but I'd want to know whether the expert has skin in the game.
resistant: I've seen 'expert' forecasts on energy costs be off by 40%. I'll trust our own analysts.
Organizations have agendas. I need primary data, not endorsements.
I've seen expert opinions on {topic} shift with the political winds. That doesn't inspire confidence.
Citing authorities on {topic} is what you do when the evidence can't stand on its own.
Experts recommending things on {topic} that benefit their own field isn't independent advice.

positive: Interesting. If our direct competitors are actually seeing cost savings, that's worth investigating.
I wasn't aware the industry had moved this fast. That does change the competitive framing.
I wasn't aware that so many people in comparable situations had moved on {topic}. That's worth investigating.

neutral: The fact that others are doing it doesn't mean it's right for us. We have different capital structures.

resistant: Plenty of companies have jumped on trends and regretted it. I need more than 'everyone's doing it'.
Following the herd is how you end up with stranded assets.
Plenty of people have followed trends on {topic} and regretted it. Popularity isn't a strategy.

positive: That's an interesting angle I hadn't considered.

neutral: I'm not sure what you're arguing exactly. Can you be more specific?

I'm not saying yes, but I'd want to see a proper pilot proposal before the next board meeting.
